free_text: The d(n,n+p) n reaction at 130 MeV, differential
cross sections from cinematically complete data over a
Protons on Be-9 target producing neutrons
with a spectrum peaked at 130 MeV with a low energy
Incident neutrons were collimated into a beam 5.9 cm
Deuterium liquid in a cryogenic container, surrounded
by deuterium gas, both inside a vacuum chamber.
Time of flight, 13.71 meter flight path.
by 12.7 cm diameter. Detectors were arranged in an
array either side of the symmetry axis, The proton
detector arms subtended an angle of 5.0 deg., 4.0msr
and the neutron arms an angle of 3.6 deg, 3.2 msr.
Valid events required coincidence between detectors.
Mass discimination was made by correlating proton
detector pulse height and proton time of flight.
This normalization was also checked with a liquid
hydrogen target and the n-p reaction cross section.
Corrected for accidental coincidences, and for the
different detection efficiencies of the charged
particle arms for deuterons and protons at various
detector efficiency and effective solid angle.
Corrections in according to Memo CP-E/089.
SD: SF7=P/N/N -> N/P/N in REACTION code.
